    Signal ChainSession Template

Studio Workflow

Signal Chain

Every instrument's path from its physical output to its Ableton Live track, including connection type, input assignment, and gain staging target.

MOTU Overview

Full Signal Routing Table

Source Output connector Cable MOTU input Ableton track type Peak target (dBFS)
Shure SM58 (vocal mic) XLR (male) XLR balanced Input 1 (front combo) Audio — Ext. In 1 (Mono) −12 to −6
Roland VT-3 Voice Transformer 1/4" Output L (mono chain exit) TRS balanced Input 2 (front combo) Audio — Ext. In 2 (Mono) −12 to −6
ASM Hydrasynth Explorer 1/4" Outputs L + R TRS balanced ×2 Inputs 3 + 4 (rear TRS) Audio — Ext. In 3/4 (Stereo) −12 to −6
Yamaha P-225 digital piano 1/4" L + R (rear) TRS balanced ×2 (or TS if unbalanced piano out) Inputs 5 + 6 (rear TRS) Audio — Ext. In 5/6 (Stereo) — or MIDI only via USB-B −12 to −6
Novation Launchkey 37 Mk3 USB-B → USB-A USB USB MIDI direct to computer MIDI — All channels n/a (MIDI only)
ROLI Seaboard M (Block M) USB-C → USB-A USB USB MIDI direct to computer MIDI — MPE (Ch 1–16) n/a (MIDI only)
Ableton Push 1 USB-B → USB-A USB USB MIDI + control surface Control surface (not a MIDI track source) n/a
Novation Launch Control XL USB Micro-B → USB-A USB USB MIDI + control surface Control surface (MIDI Remote Script) n/a
Knobbler USB-C → USB-A USB USB MIDI direct to computer MIDI CC → mapped parameters (no dedicated track) n/a
Roland VT-3 (MIDI) USB-B MIDI USB USB MIDI direct to computer MIDI Out track (for pitch / effect control via CC) n/a
Yamaha HS7 monitors 1/4" TRS balanced inputs TRS balanced ×2 MOTU Main Out 1 + 2 Master bus output n/a (output)
Headphones 1/4" TRS (front panel) 1/4" TRS MOTU Phones Out (front) CueMix mix bus (assignable) n/a (output)

MIDI Routing Notes

  • Hydrasynth Explorer MIDI DIN: connect MOTU MIDI Out → Hydrasynth MIDI In for DAW-sequenced control and clock sync. For standalone play, the Hydrasynth is its own MIDI controller via USB or DIN.
  • Yamaha P-225 DIN MIDI: MOTU MIDI Out → P-225 MIDI In for program-change and transport commands from Ableton when using the P-225 as a soft synth controller.
  • Launchkey 37 Mk3 Standalone: if the computer is off, use the Launchkey's MIDI Out (5-pin DIN) → Hydrasynth MIDI In for hardware-only performance.
  • MPE routing (Seaboard M): Seaboard M USB → computer → Ableton MIDI track input; set MIDI track to All Channels + MPE. Use the M4L MPE Control device if routing into a non-MPE instrument.

Audio Bus Structure in Ableton

  • Vocal bus: SM58 (Ext. In 1) → Vocal Audio track → EQ Eight → Compressor → VT-3 Return (Ext. In 2 on a second track or Send/Return from the VT-3's Audio Out)
  • Synth bus: Hydrasynth (Ext. In 3/4) → Hydrasynth Audio track → sends to Reverb and Delay Return tracks
  • Piano bus: P-225 (Ext. In 5/6) → Piano Audio track (or MIDI track via External Instrument → instrument)
  • Master chain: all buses → Master track → Glue Compressor → EQ Eight (reference check) → out to MOTU Main Out 1/2 → HS7 monitors

Built for fast lookups, cleaner routing decisions, and practical studio recall.
